_meta.description: Seed work-order requests for vendor-discovery v0. Each entry pairs the raw facility-manager chat (as a monolithic ground-truth message AND as a phased sequence of user turns) with the structured work_order that intake would have extracted from the conversation. Vendor discovery consumes the work_order to find + score candidate vendors; the chat payload is retained for intake-LLM regression testing (can it extract correctly from a single shot? can it ask the right follow-ups when info arrives in pieces?) and for downstream grounding.

schema_notes.chat.message: Monolithic single-shot version — everything the FM would say if they typed it all at once. Use as ground truth for extraction coverage.
schema_notes.chat.turns: Phased customer-only messages from the frontend chatbox, timestamped. No bot turns included — the intake LLM's job is to decide what to ask between turns. Content across all turns contains the same information as chat.message.

chat.message: Hey, this is Jordan over at FM Holdings. I've got an active leak at our Walmart at 2304 Stemmons Trail in Dallas. Water is pouring out of the wall by the women's restroom. The store manager, Maria Hernandez, just called me — it started maybe 20 minutes ago. I need someone out there ASAP, within the next two hours if possible. Store closes at 11 PM so we need this sorted before we get deeper. I'm willing to pay emergency rate, up to about fifteen hundred dollars. Vendor needs to be licensed and insured, this is commercial. Maria will be on site until close. Have them pull around to the dock at the rear for parking.

turns.content: emergency — active water leak at our dallas walmart. need someone out ASAP
2304 stemmons trail. water pouring from the wall by the womens restroom, started maybe 20 min ago
willing to pay emergency rate up to $1500. store closes at 11pm so needs to be fixed before then
has to be licensed + insured, this is commercial. store mgr maria hernandez onsite til close — have them pull around to the rear dock for parking

work_order.description: Active water leak from wall near women's restroom at commercial Walmart location; needs immediate repair before 11pm close.
work_order.access_notes: Manager Maria Hernandez on-site until 11pm close; vendor parking at rear dock.

chat.message: Setting up Q2 drain maintenance for our Charlotte headquarters at 5950 Fairview Rd, SouthPark area. About 14,000 square feet, 8 bathrooms across floors 2 and 3. Last vendor charged us around $650 for the full sweep last quarter. Want to book for next Tuesday, April 28th, ideally starting after 2 PM so it doesn't disrupt morning traffic. Budget's $800. Not a big job so I don't need a master plumber but they need to be insured — we can't have anyone in the building uncovered. Night manager can give access after 6 PM if Tuesday afternoon doesn't work.

turns.content: hi — need to schedule q2 drain maintenance at our charlotte HQ
address is 5950 fairview rd, southpark area. ~14k sq ft, 8 bathrooms across floors 2 and 3
last vendor did the full sweep for $650 last quarter. budget is $800 this time. want to book for next tuesday apr 28, ideally starting after 2pm so it doesnt disrupt morning traffic
dont need a master plumber for this but they have to be insured — we cant have anyone in the building uncovered. if tues afternoon doesnt work, night manager can give access after 6pm

work_order.description: Quarterly drain maintenance sweep across 8 bathrooms in 14,000 sqft Charlotte corporate HQ.
work_order.access_notes: After-hours access via night manager at security desk; bathrooms on floors 2 and 3.

chat.message: Emergency — our Phoenix restaurant's AC unit is dead. We're at 2825 N Central Ave. It's 108 degrees outside, servers are dripping, customers are walking out mid-meal. Need someone here TODAY no matter the cost. Up to $3,500 for a same-day fix. Restaurant's open till 10 PM. Has to be licensed — commercial HVAC. Manager is Brett, he'll be on-site till close. Enter from the E McDowell side, rear alley for deliveries and equipment.

turns.content: our phoenix restaurant AC unit is dead and its 108 outside. customers are literally walking out mid-meal
2825 N Central Ave. need someone TODAY no matter the cost. up to $3500 for same-day fix. open til 10pm
has to be licensed, commercial HVAC. manager brett onsite til close. enter from the E McDowell side, rear alley for equipment

work_order.description: AC unit completely down at Phoenix restaurant during 108F heat; customers leaving; same-day commercial HVAC fix needed.
work_order.access_notes: Manager Brett on-site till 10pm; enter from E McDowell side, equipment via rear alley.

chat.message: Looking to schedule Q2 HVAC preventative maintenance at our Five Below at 1163 Howell Mill Rd NW, Atlanta. Two rooftop units, roughly 5 tons each, both pretty standard. Want it done within the next 2-3 weeks, ideally a weekday morning before we open at 10 AM. Budget around $400. Insured required, license preferred but not a hard blocker. Rooftop access is via interior ladder at the receiving area, and our building manager opens at 7 AM on weekdays.

The refrigeration panel is in the back-of-house closet, left of the produce cooler. Have them check in at customer service desk.

chat.message: Outfitting a new Anytime Fitness at 12300 N Lamar Blvd, Austin. Construction wraps in about 2 weeks (target Apr 30). Need an electrician on-site the following Monday, May 4th, 8 AM start. Scope: install 14 dedicated 20A circuits for cardio equipment and 4 240V outlets for the hardline machines. Should be a full-day job. Estimating $4,500 based on bids we got for the previous build. Licensed required, TX master electrician preferred. Site is mid-construction so coordinate with our GC Jared Wynn, 512-555-0117. Drawings in the trailer on site.
